weakest_questions.distractor: The team would need to use a cloud platform such as AWS SageMaker JumpStart to manage the fine-tuning workflow.
weakest_questions.note: Weak distractor — most beginners would not perceive platform availability as a disqualifying factor. Plausible but not a strong misconception.

learning_objectives: Define fine-tuning in plain language and explain how it differs from training a model from scratch.
Explain what domain-specific data means and give two examples of a domain and its corresponding data.
Describe the general sequence of steps involved in fine-tuning a foundation model.
Identify at least two real-world scenarios where fine-tuning is the appropriate adaptation choice.
Explain the trade-off between fine-tuning and using a foundation model without any adaptation (zero-shot use).
Recognise when fine-tuning may not be the right tool — for example, when the task changes frequently or when labelled data is unavailable.

notes: All stems use concrete scenario framing accessible to freshers.
No unexplained acronyms in stems or answer options.
AWS SageMaker JumpStart in q6 distractor is defined/introduced in corpus §6 — in-scope.
q4 distractor referencing post-cutoff information implies retrieval-based approaches without naming or defining RAG — mention-and-defer compliant.
All correct answers and distractors are grounded in concepts taught in this topic or in the declared prerequisite (4.1).
Advisory (soft): q5 correct answer is dense (238 chars, two-clause trade-off) — comprehension load is high for a beginner but the content is accurate and on-scope.
